tools.name: email_list
tools.description: List Gmail message metadata for a label, ordered newest-first by internalDate (the natural inbox semantic). Returns one row per message — the id, thread_id, subject, from, snippet, internalDate, and label_ids — NOT the full body. Use email_read for the body. When `project_id` is supplied, results are filtered to messages also carrying the `Neutron/<project_id>` user label.

label.description: Gmail label id (e.g. 'INBOX', 'IMPORTANT', 'UNREAD', or a user label). Defaults to 'INBOX'.

max_results.description: Maximum messages to return. Backend default applies when omitted.

page_token.description: Opaque cursor returned by a prior call. Omit on the first page.

project_id.description: Optional project scope. Filters results to messages tagged with `Neutron/<project_id>`.

internal_date.description: ISO-8601 datetime derived from Gmail's epoch-ms internalDate.

tools.name: email_read
tools.description: Fetch the full body and headers of a single Gmail message by id.

tools.name: email_search
tools.description: Run a Gmail-style query (e.g. 'from:alice@x.com is:unread', 'subject:invoice newer_than:7d') and return matching message metadata, ordered newest-first. When `project_id` is supplied, `label:Neutron/<project_id>` is appended automatically.

query.description: Gmail search query — the same syntax the Gmail web UI accepts.

project_id.description: Optional project scope; AND-s `label:Neutron/<project_id>` into the query.

tools.name: email_summarize
tools.description: Summarize a single Gmail message. Returns a structured row (from, subject, key_points[], sentiment, ask_or_response). When `as_brief:true`, ALSO returns a 2-3 sentence prose brief composed by the Haiku-fast summarizer on top of the structured row.

thread_id.description: Optional thread context. v1 ignores this field — the summarizer summarizes the single message identified by message_id.

as_brief.description: When true, compose a 2-3 sentence prose brief over the structured row.

tools.name: email_draft_prepare
tools.description: Create a Gmail DRAFT (drafts.create) and atomically apply the owner 4-point label policy (INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD) to the resulting thread via threads.modify. When `project_id` is supplied, ALSO applies the `Neutron/<project_id>` user-label. NOT sending — the draft lands in the user's Drafts label for review. Returns the new draft id + the persisted message metadata + the applied labels list. Sending is intentionally Tier 2 (Email-Private paid Core); this Tier 1 free Core only prepares drafts.

reply_to_message_id.description: Optional — when set, the draft is linked to the existing Gmail thread of this message (In-Reply-To / References headers populated server-side).

project_id.description: Optional project scope; AND-s `Neutron/<project_id>` to the draft's thread labels.

message_id.description: Id of the underlying draft message resource (drafts.message.id). Distinct from draft_id.

applied_labels.description: Labels applied to the draft's thread — always includes INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD; includes Neutron/<project_id> when project_id was supplied.

tools.name: email_triage
tools.description: Run the Haiku-fast triage agent over the most recent N inbox messages and return a top-5 ranked list with one-line reasons. When `dry_run:true`, returns the triage WITHOUT posting to chat. When `project_id` is supplied, only messages tagged with `Neutron/<project_id>` are considered.

project_id.description: Optional project scope; restricts the triage to messages tagged with Neutron/<project_id>.

dry_run.description: When true, compose the triage but do NOT post to chat.

tools.name: email_send
tools.description: Send an email via Gmail messages.send. Sends a NEW message, or a reply when `reply_to_message_id` is supplied (In-Reply-To / References headers + thread attachment are populated server-side). After sending, the owner visibility labels (INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD) are applied to the sent thread so the conversation surfaces in the owner's inbox — the send-path counterpart to the 4-point draft rule. When `project_id` is supplied, the `Neutron/<project_id>` user-label is ALSO applied. Header values containing CR/LF/NUL are rejected (header-injection guard). Returns the sent message id, thread id, and the applied labels.

reply_to_message_id.description: Optional — when set, the message is sent as a reply on the existing Gmail thread of this message.

project_id.description: Optional project scope; AND-s `Neutron/<project_id>` onto the sent thread's labels.

applied_labels.description: Visibility labels applied to the sent thread — always includes INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD; includes Neutron/<project_id> when project_id was supplied.

linked_sources.description: Gmail v1 — read messages, create drafts, and send mail on the user's Gmail account. The Core requests FOUR Google OAuth scopes: `gmail.readonly` (list/read/search/summarize/triage reach into the inbox), `gmail.modify` (the owner visibility label policy applies INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD to every drafted/sent thread via threads.modify), `gmail.compose` (drafts.create), and `gmail.send` (messages.send for the email_send tool). The 4-point DRAFT rule (DRAFT + INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD) is enforced on every draft; send applies the same INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD visibility labels to the sent thread.

secrets.name: gmail_managed_oauth
secrets.label: gmail_compose
secrets.install_prompt: Connect your Gmail account so Neutron can read messages (gmail.readonly), label drafted/sent threads INBOX + IMPORTANT + UNREAD (gmail.modify), prepare drafts in your Drafts label (gmail.compose), and send mail on your behalf (gmail.send).
